annotations.label: Definition of the tangent bundle (disjoint union of tangent spaces)

anchor.snippet: the tangent bundle of a differentiable manifold

annotations.note: `TangentBundle I M` is defined as `Bundle.TotalSpace E (TangentSpace I : M Рєњ Type _)`, i.e. the sigma-type / disjoint union of the tangent spaces `TangentSpace I x` (each a type synonym for the model space `E`).

annotations.label: Elements of the tangent bundle as point-vector pairs

anchor.snippet: denotes the tangent space to

annotations.note: Elements of `TangentBundle I M` are `Bundle.TotalSpace` points `РЪеx, vРЪЕ` with `x : M` and `v : TangentSpace I x`, accessed via `.proj`/`.1` (base point) and `.2` (tangent vector).

annotations.label: Natural projection of the tangent bundle

anchor.snippet: There is a natural projection

annotations.note: The bundle projection `¤ђ E (TangentSpace I)` / `TotalSpace.proj` maps `РЪеx, vРЪЕ Рєд x`; its continuity/smoothness is `FiberBundle.continuous_proj` and `contMDiff_proj` (Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.VectorBundle.Basic).

annotations.label: A section of the tangent bundle is a vector field; cotangent bundle is the dual

anchor.snippet: is a vector field on

annotations.note: A vector field is a (smooth) section of the tangent bundle: `ContMDiffSection I E n (TangentSpace I)`, or unbundled `╬а x : M, TangentSpace I x`. The cotangent bundle exists only implicitly as `╬а x : M, TangentSpace I x РєњL[­ЮЋю] ­ЮЋю` (see `extDerivFun`); there is no named `CotangentBundle`/dual-bundle declaration.

annotations.label: A manifold is parallelizable iff its tangent bundle is trivial

anchor.snippet: is parallelizable if and only if the tangent bundle is trivial

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `Parallelizable` predicate and no theorem relating it to triviality of the tangent bundle. The closest infrastructure is local frames (`IsLocalFrameOn`, Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.VectorBundle.LocalFrame) and the trivial bundle `Bundle.Trivial`, but no global-frame / parallelizability notion.

annotations.label: A manifold is framed iff its tangent bundle is stably trivial

anchor.snippet: is framed if and only if the tangent bundle

annotations.note: No notion of framed manifold or stable triviality of a bundle in Mathlib. `Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.Bordism` exists but does not define framings or stably-trivial tangent bundles.

annotations.label: The n-sphere is framed for all n but parallelizable only for n = 1, 3, 7

anchor.snippet: sphere S n is framed for all n , but parallelizable only for

annotations.note: Not formalized. Mathlib defines spheres as manifolds (`Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.Instances.Sphere`) but neither parallelizability of S^1/S^3/S^7 nor framings of S^n are present; the underlying parallelizability/framing notions do not exist.

annotations.label: Derivative of a smooth map is a smooth map between tangent bundles

anchor.snippet: its derivative is a smooth function

annotations.note: `tangentMap I I' f : TangentBundle I M Рєњ TangentBundle I' M'` is the bundled derivative; `ContMDiff.contMDiff_tangentMap` shows that if `f` is `C^n` then `tangentMap f` is `C^m` for `m + 1 РЅц n` (and `ContMDiff.continuous_tangentMap` for continuity).

annotations.label: The dimension of the tangent bundle is twice the dimension of the manifold

anchor.snippet: is twice the dimension of

annotations.note: The tangent bundle is charted on `ModelProd H E` (so locally `H ├Ќ E`, dimension `2 dim M` in the finite-dimensional case), but there is no `finrank`/dimension theorem stating `dim (TangentBundle I M) = 2 * dim M`.

annotations.label: Over an open contractible subset the tangent bundle is diffeomorphic to a product

anchor.snippet: is an open contractible subset of

annotations.note: Not formalized. The tangent bundle is locally trivial over chart domains (`trivializationAt`), but there is no result that over a contractible open set it is diffeomorphic to a product (which would require triviality of bundles over contractible bases).

annotations.label: Definition of a trivial tangent bundle

anchor.snippet: then the tangent bundle is said to be trivial

annotations.note: `Bundle.Trivial B F` is the trivial bundle (with `Bundle.Trivial.fiberBundle`/`vectorBundle` instances), but Mathlib has no predicate asserting that a *given* bundle (e.g. a particular `TangentBundle I M`) is isomorphic to a trivial one, so 'the tangent bundle is trivial' is not directly expressible.

annotations.label: Definition of parallelizable manifold (trivial tangent bundle)

anchor.snippet: which have a trivial tangent bundle are called parallelizable

annotations.note: No `Parallelizable` definition in Mathlib (duplicate of the lead annotation on parallelizability).

annotations.label: The tangent bundle of the unit circle is trivial (it is a Lie group)

anchor.snippet: The tangent bundle of the unit circle is trivial because it is a Lie group

annotations.note: Mathlib has `instance : LieGroup (­ЮЊА 1) ¤Ѕ Circle` (Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.Instances.Sphere), and a general fact that Lie groups have trivial tangent bundles would follow from the Lie-algebra trivialization, but neither the trivialization of `TM` for a Lie group nor triviality of the circle's tangent bundle is stated.

annotations.label: Topology and smooth structure on the tangent bundle via coordinate charts

anchor.snippet: We use these maps to define the topology and smooth structure on

annotations.note: `tangentBundleCore I M : VectorBundleCore ­ЮЋю M E (atlas H M)` builds the topology and (for `C^{n+1}` `M`) the `C^n` vector-bundle/manifold structure on `TangentBundle I M` via the chart-indexed coordinate changes (Jacobians `fderivWithin` of `extend`-coordinate changes); the topology instance and `TangentBundle.chartAt` come from this.

annotations.label: The tangent bundle as a rank-n vector bundle with Jacobian transition functions

anchor.snippet: may be defined as a rank

annotations.note: `TangentSpace.vectorBundle` gives `VectorBundle ­ЮЋю E (TangentSpace I)`, with fiber the model space `E` and transition functions the Jacobian derivatives of coordinate changes (`tangentBundleCore.coordChange = fderivWithin ...`), matching the rank-n (= dim E) vector bundle with Jacobian transitions.

annotations.label: Tangent bundle of R^n is trivial

anchor.snippet: In this case the tangent bundle is trivial

annotations.note: For the model space `H` (e.g. `E = R^n` with `I = ­ЮЊў(­ЮЋю, E)`), `tangentBundleModelSpaceHomeomorph : TangentBundle I H РЅЃРѓю ModelProd H E` exhibits the tangent bundle as homeomorphic to the product, and the trivialization is the identity (`trivializationAt_model_space_apply`). This is the model-space triviality rather than a general 'TM of R^n is trivial' statement.

annotations.label: Tangent bundle of the unit circle is trivial (an infinite cylinder)

anchor.snippet: The tangent bundle of the circle is also trivial

annotations.note: Not formalized; same gap as the earlier circle annotation. Mathlib has the circle as a manifold/Lie group but no statement that its tangent bundle is trivial (a cylinder).

annotations.label: Only the tangent bundles of the line and circle are readily visualized

anchor.snippet: The only tangent bundles that can be readily visualized

annotations.note: Informal expository remark with no mathematical content to formalize.

annotations.label: Tangent bundle of the unit sphere is nontrivial (hairy ball theorem)

anchor.snippet: this tangent bundle is nontrivial as a consequence of the hairy ball theorem

annotations.note: Not formalized: there is no hairy ball theorem in Mathlib and no statement that the tangent bundle of S^2 (or S^{2n}) is nontrivial / admits no nonvanishing vector field.

annotations.label: Definition of a vector field as a smooth map into the tangent bundle

anchor.snippet: a vector field on a manifold

annotations.note: A smooth vector field is `CРѓЏ^nРЪ«I; E, TangentSpace IРЪ»` = `ContMDiffSection I E n (TangentSpace I)`, a `C^n` section assigning `x Рєд v x Рѕѕ TangentSpace I x`; unbundled vector fields appear as `╬а x, TangentSpace I x` throughout `Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.VectorField`.

annotations.label: A vector field is a section of the tangent bundle

anchor.snippet: such a map is called a section

annotations.note: `ContMDiffSection` is exactly a section `s : ╬а x, V x` (with `s.toFun` satisfying `proj Рѕў s = id`) that is `C^n`; applied to `V = TangentSpace I` it is a vector field. Continuity of the projection makes the section condition `¤ђ Рѕў s = id`.

annotations.label: The set of vector fields is a module over the algebra of smooth functions

anchor.snippet: takes on the structure of a module over the commutative algebra

annotations.note: `ContMDiffSection.instModule` gives the module structure of sections over the scalar field `­ЮЋю` (with scalar multiplication by `C^n` functions `¤ѕ Рђб s` also defined). The module structure over the full algebra `C^Рѕъ(M, ­ЮЋю)` of smooth functions is not registered as an instance, so only the field-module structure is formalized.

annotations.label: Definition of a local vector field (local section) and the sheaf they form

anchor.snippet: A local vector field on

annotations.note: Local/within-a-set vector field notions appear (e.g. `mlieBracketWithin` for vector fields on a set), and local frames (`IsLocalFrameOn`) exist, but there is no `StructureSheaf`/sheaf-of-vector-fields object assembling local sections into a sheaf.

annotations.label: Differential 1-forms as sections of the cotangent bundle

anchor.snippet: are precisely the sections of the cotangent bundle

annotations.note: A 1-form is represented as a section `╬а x, TangentSpace I x РєњL[­ЮЋю] ­ЮЋю` of the (implicit) cotangent bundle; `extDerivFun g` produces the exterior derivative of a scalar function as such a section. There is no named `CotangentBundle` nor a general space of differential 1-forms / differential forms.

annotations.label: Definition of the second-order tangent bundle

anchor.snippet: the second-order tangent bundle can be defined via repeated application

annotations.note: Since `TangentBundle I M` is itself a `C^n` manifold (via `IsManifold (IB.prod ­ЮЊў(­ЮЋю,F)) n (TotalSpace ...)` and model `I.tangent`), the double tangent bundle `TangentBundle I.tangent (TangentBundle I M)` is expressible, but there is no dedicated `SecondOrderTangentBundle` definition.

annotations.label: Recursive definition of the k-th order tangent bundle

anchor.snippet: th order tangent bundle

annotations.note: No recursive/iterated k-th order tangent bundle definition in Mathlib; while iterating `TangentBundle` is in principle possible (each is again a manifold), the general indexed construction is not formalized.

annotations.label: Higher-order tangent bundles are domain and range for higher-order derivatives

anchor.snippet: higher-order tangent bundles provide the domain and range for higher-order derivatives

annotations.note: Not formalized as such. Higher-order derivatives of maps between manifolds are handled via iterated `mfderiv`/`mfderivWithin` and `ContMDiff`, not via maps between higher-order tangent bundles.

annotations.label: Jet bundles as bundles of jets on a manifold

anchor.snippet: are the jet bundles on a manifold

annotations.note: No jet bundle definition in Mathlib; '1-jet' is mentioned only informally in docstrings (Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.ContMDiffMFDeriv, .VectorBundle.CovariantDerivative.Basic), with no `JetBundle`/`Jet` object.

annotations.label: Definition of the canonical vector field on the tangent bundle (diagonal map)

anchor.section: Canonical vector field on tangent bundle
anchor.snippet: one can define a canonical vector field

annotations.note: No canonical / Liouville / Euler vector field on the tangent bundle is defined. The only 'canonical' mention near tangent spaces refers to `NormedSpace.fromTangentSpace`, an unrelated identification.

annotations.label: The double tangent bundle is locally a product of a curved and a flat factor

anchor.section: Canonical vector field on tangent bundle
anchor.snippet: Thus the tangent bundle of the tangent bundle is locally

annotations.note: Not formalized. The double tangent bundle is expressible (TM is a manifold) but there is no result describing its local product structure (curved horizontal factor times flat vertical factor).

annotations.label: Local coordinate expression of the canonical vector field

anchor.section: Canonical vector field on tangent bundle
anchor.snippet: the vector field has the expression

annotations.note: Not formalized; depends on the canonical vector field, which is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Canonical vector field as derivative of scalar multiplication at t = 0

anchor.section: Canonical vector field on tangent bundle
anchor.snippet: which is an alternative description of the canonical vector field

annotations.note: Not formalized; the canonical vector field and its derivative-of-scaling description are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Axiomatic characterization of the tangent bundle via the canonical vector field

anchor.section: Canonical vector field on tangent bundle
anchor.snippet: can be characterized using 4 axioms

annotations.note: Not formalized; no axiomatic characterization of the tangent bundle (via the canonical vector field or otherwise) exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tangent lift of a curve into the double tangent bundle

anchor.snippet: is a curve in

annotations.note: No tangent-lift-of-a-curve construction. Mathlib has integral curves of vector fields (`Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.IntegralCurve`) but not the lift of a curve into the double tangent bundle.

annotations.label: Vertical lift of a function

anchor.snippet: The vertical lift of a function

annotations.note: No vertical lift (of a function or vector field) defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: SMOKE2_HUMAN Рђћ preservation check

anchor.snippet: tangent bundle

annotations.note: Smoke2 human annotation Рђћ should survive verbatim.

annotations.label: SMOKE2_AI_MODERATED Рђћ carry-through check

anchor.snippet: vector field

annotations.note: Smoke2 ai-moderated annotation Рђћ provenance should NOT be downgraded by Agent 2.

annotations.label: The tangent space at a point

anchor.snippet: where [MATH] denotes the tangent space to

annotations.note: `TangentSpace I x` is the fiber of the tangent bundle at `x : M`, defined as a type synonym for the model space `E` carrying the normed-space structure pulled back via the chart at `x`.

annotations.label: Pointwise addition of vector fields

anchor.snippet: Vector fields can be added together pointwise

annotations.note: Pointwise addition of `C^n` sections (including vector fields) is the `Add` instance on `ContMDiffSection`, with `(s + t) x = s x + t x` in the fiber `TangentSpace I x`.

annotations.label: Scalar multiplication of a vector field by a smooth function

anchor.snippet: and multiplied by smooth functions on M

annotations.note: Multiplication of a section by a smooth scalar function is available via the `SMul` action of `C^n` functions on `ContMDiffSection`, giving another `C^n` section/vector field; the full `C^Рѕъ(M,­ЮЋю)`-module instance is not registered.
